science.title: Breakthrough discovery reveals hidden oxygen flow deep inside catalysts
science.summary: A major discovery is reshaping how scientists think about catalysts. Researchers have, for the first time, captured oxygen atoms moving through the interior of a catalyst—not just along its surface. This reveals that the bulk material can actively participate in reactions, opening a new frontier in catalyst design. The

science.title: This donut-shaped discovery just shattered a 150-year math rule
science.summary: A 150-year-old rule in geometry has been proven wrong. Mathematicians found two different doughnut-shaped surfaces that look identical when measured locally but are actually different overall. For decades, researchers suspected this might be possible but couldn’t prove it—until now. The breakthrough reshapes how mathem

science.title: This new camera captures what happens in a trillionth of a second
science.summary: Scientists have unveiled a breakthrough imaging method that can capture the hidden details of events unfolding in trillionths of a second. This new technique doesn’t just track how bright something is—it also reveals subtle structural changes that were previously invisible, all in a single shot. By effectively turning

science.title: These tiny dinosaur fossils fooled scientists for 20 years
science.summary: Tiny dinosaur fossils that puzzled scientists for over 20 years have finally revealed their true identity. Rather than belonging to a miniature species, they are actually baby ankylosaurs—some less than a year old, including a possible hatchling. By studying bone growth patterns, researchers confirmed these young dinos

science.title: 95% of people carry this virus and scientists may have just found how to stop it
science.summary: Scientists have taken a major step toward stopping Epstein-Barr virus (EBV), an extremely common infection linked to cancer and chronic disease. By using mice engineered with human antibody genes, researchers created powerful human-like antibodies that block the virus from attaching to and entering immune cells. One of

tech.title: Tesla Q1 revenue rises, driven by EV sales and FSD subscriptions
tech.summary: Sales rebounded a bit from the first quarter of 2025, as Tesla throws a ton of money at massive bets like robotics, AI, and its own chip fab.

tech.title: OpenAI teams up with Infosys to bring AI tools to more businesses
tech.summary: Infosys said the integration will be used to help its clients modernize software development, automate workflows, and deploy AI systems, initially focusing software engineering, legacy modernization, and DevOps.

tech.title: Google unveils two new TPUs designed for the "agentic era"
tech.summary: Google's new generation of Tensor AI chips is actually two chips, one for inference and one for training.

tech.title: Elon Musk admits millions of Tesla owners need upgrades for true ‘Full Self-Driving’
tech.summary: The admission could open Tesla to legal challenges after it spent years promising customers they were just one software update away from owning fully autonomous cars.

tech.title: Google Cloud launches two new AI chips to compete with Nvidia
tech.summary: Google's newest TPUs are faster and cheaper than the previous versions. But the company is still embracing Nvidia in its cloud — for now.

medicine.title: Aural symptoms common among patients with vestibular migraine, Meniere disease
medicine.summary: Patients with vestibular migraine (VM) often have aural symptoms and these are predictive of a lower probability of symptom improvement for headache and dizziness, according to a study published online Feb. 24 in the Journal of Clinical Medicine.

medicine.title: Hidden virus in your gut may be linked to colon cancer
medicine.summary: A newly discovered virus hiding inside a common gut bacterium could help explain one of medicine’s long-standing mysteries: why a microbe found in both healthy people and cancer patients is linked to colorectal cancer. The research suggests that the interaction between bacteria and the viruses they carry may be key to

medicine.title: Two common drugs may reverse fatty liver disease, study finds
medicine.summary: Scientists have discovered that combining two existing drugs can dramatically reduce liver fat linked to a common and often silent disease. The treatment not only improved liver health in animal models but also showed potential to lower heart-related risks. Interestingly, using lower doses of both drugs together worked

medicine.title: This cheap drug could help people with type 1 diabetes use less insulin
medicine.summary: A surprising new clinical trial has revealed that metformin—a cheap, century-old drug widely used for type 2 diabetes—may help people with type 1 diabetes in an unexpected way. While researchers initially hoped it would reduce insulin resistance, they instead found it allows patients to use about 12% less insulin while

medicine.title: 95% of people carry this virus and scientists may have just found how to stop it
medicine.summary: Scientists have taken a major step toward stopping Epstein-Barr virus (EBV), an extremely common infection linked to cancer and chronic disease. By using mice engineered with human antibody genes, researchers created powerful human-like antibodies that block the virus from attaching to and entering immune cells. One of

mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. The post In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far? first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere
mathematics.summary: The central limit theorem started as a bar trick for 18th-century gamblers. Now scientists rely on it every day. The post The Math That Explains Why Bell Curves Are Everywhere first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: School of Science welcomed new faculty in 2024
mathematics.summary: Eleven new professors join the departments of Biology; Brain and Cognitive Sciences; Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences; Mathematics; and Physics.

mathematics.title: Laurent Demanet appointed co-director of MIT Center for Computational Science and Engineering
mathematics.summary: Applied mathematics professor will join fellow co-director Nicolas Hadjiconstantinou in leading the cross-cutting center.

space.title: Artemis II Mission Milestones: An Image and Video Recap
space.summary: On April 1, 2026, Artemis II launched on a nearly 10-day voyage around the Moon, marking the first crewed flight of NASA’s Orion spacecraft. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, along with CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen, splashed down on April 10

space.title: Watch Rocket Lab launch Japanese 'origami' satellite, 7 other spacecraft to orbit tonight
space.summary: Rocket Lab will launch eight Japanese satellites to orbit tonight (April 22), including one that features an origami-folded antenna, and you can watch the action live.

space.title: Celebrate Earth Day with this Artemis 2 shot | Space photo of the day for April 22, 2026
space.summary: Celebrating Earth Day with an image of Earth captured by NASA's Artemis 2 mission around the moon.

space.title: You've heard of the Space Force. Now there's a 'NASA Force' — but it's not about making space war
space.summary: NASA Force aims to recruit industry talent to the space agency for relatively short stints. Applications are open now in the wake of the Artemis 2 moon mission.

space.title: 600 rocket landings! SpaceX notches another milestone during Sunday Starlink launch (video)
space.summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 25 Starlink satellites lifted off from Vandenberg Space Force Station in California on April 19. The mission featured the 600th landing of a Falcon first stage.

astronomy.title: NASA’s SPHEREx Telescope Just Mapped the Cosmic Ices That Will Someday Build Planets
astronomy.summary: New missions mean new capabilities - and one particularly interesting new mission is finally up and running. Data is starting to come in from SPHEREx, the medium-class surveyor that is mapping the entire sky every six months. A paper based on some of that early data was recently published in The Astrophysical Journal,

astronomy.title: Planets Collide Around Young, Sun-like Star
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have uncovered evidence that two planets collided around a young star, revealing how giant impacts sculpt baby solar systems. The post Planets Collide Around Young, Sun-like Star appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: How a Black Hole and a Shredded Star Could Light Up a Galaxy
astronomy.summary: In 2014, a strange cloudy object called G2 made a close approach to Sagittarius A*, (Sag A*) the supermassive black hole at the heart of the Milky Way Galaxy. Astronomers were pretty excited, partly because they thought it might get torn apart by Sag A*'s intense gravitational pull. That didn't happen, and the event wa

astronomy.title: Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way
astronomy.summary: Astronomers have located the edge of the Milky Way’s star-forming disk for the first time, showing that star formation is focused within 40,000 light-years of our galactic center. The post Astronomers Find the Edge of the Milky Way appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Tracking Changes in the Trifid Nebula With the Hubble
astronomy.summary: Back in 1997, the Hubble Space Telescope imaged the spectacular Trifid Nebula, a region of active star-formation. Now the telescope has revisited the Trifid. By comparing both images, astronomers have tracked some changes that tell them about how young stars behave and evolve.

architecture.title: Reclaiming the Street: Alejandra Ferrera on Architecture and Urban Life in Honduras
architecture.summary: Honduras is the second-largest country in Central America, both in territory and population. Today, its urban fabric remains heavily influenced by modernist principles from the 1970s that prioritised high-speed arterial corridors and automobile-dependent "point-to-point" mobility. architecture.title: Gensler set to design Trump Tower Tbilisi as country's tallest building
architecture.summary: The Trump Organization has announced plans to build a 70-storey skyscraper in Tbilisi, Georgia, which would be the tallest building in the country. Planned in collaboration with Biograpi Living, Archi Group, The Sapir Organization, Blox Group and Finvest Georgia, the skyscraper is set to be designed by US architecture

architecture.title: Five Texas cities "catalyzing" urban development with convention centres
architecture.summary: Nearly $9 billion of convention centre design and construction is currently underway in Texas's five biggest cities. Local writer Kate Mazade asks experts about the phenomenon and dives into the plans for each city. From tactical renovations and expansions to complete demolitions and rebuilds, the Lone Star State is ex

architecture.title: Flower-covered theatre forms centrepiece of Denise Scott Brown's photography exhibition
architecture.summary: A flower-covered theatre has been installed at the centre of an exhibition dedicated to architect Denise Scott Brown's photographs at the brutalist Yale architecture school building in New Haven, USA. Set within the Paul Rudolph-designed architecture school, Encounters: Denise Scott Brown Photographs aims to draw atten

architecture.title: ZGF wraps space shuttle in stainless-steel tower for California Science Center
architecture.summary: American architecture studio ZGF's building in Los Angeles is complete with a full-size space shuttle "in launch position" wrapped in a stainless steel cylinder. The Samuel Oschin Air and Space Center (Air and Space Center), designed by ZGF Architects and built by MATT Construction, took four years to complete. It is p

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Correction: Wrong Casino Collapse Photo
construction.summary: Related Links: Engineering News Record Architectural Record ﻿The story "OSHA Points to Process Errors in Cincinnati Casino Collapse" contained errors. construction.title: Consigli CIO sees biggest AI impacts in estimating
construction.summary: At the Milford, Massachusetts-based construction company, AI is useful for tracking revisions and trade offs. Its use has become an expectation for workers, says Anthony Chiaradonna.

construction.title: Construction M&A activity continues in 2026
construction.summary: Four contech giants added to their offerings while two builders prepared to strengthen their presence in growing markets.

construction.title: $1B Virginia airport upgrade project reaches milestone
construction.summary: W.M. Jordan served as construction manager at risk for Norfolk International Airport’s three new gates in Concourse A, an addition spanning nearly 19,000 square feet.

ai.title: Quantum inspired qubit qutrit neural networks for real time financial forecasting
ai.summary: arXiv:2604.18838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This research investigates the performance and efficacy of machine learning models in stock prediction, comparing Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs), Quantum Qubit-based Neural Networks (QQBNs), and Quantum Qutrit-based Neural Networks (QQTNs). ai.title: Explicit Trait Inference for Multi-Agent Coordination
ai.summary: arXiv:2604.19278v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based multi-agent systems (MAS) show promise on complex tasks but remain prone to coordination failures such as goal drift, error cascades, and misaligned behaviors. ai.title: Large Language Models Exhibit Normative Conformity
ai.summary: arXiv:2604.19301v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The conformity bias exhibited by large language models (LLMs) can pose a significant challenge to decision-making in LLM-based multi-agent systems (LLM-MAS). ai.title: Do Agents Dream of Root Shells? Partial-Credit Evaluation of LLM Agents in Capture The Flag Challenges
ai.summary: arXiv:2604.19354v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly proposed for autonomous cybersecurity tasks, but their capabilities in realistic offensive settings remain poorly understood.
